steps.title: Identify the slope magnitude
steps.content:
The scatterplot shows a steep negative correlation. Moving from $x = 1$ to $x = 8$, the $y$-values decline from approximately 90 to approximately 20—a drop of 70 units across 7 units of $x$.

A slope of $-0.1$ (choices B and C) would change $y$ by only 0.1 per unit of $x$. Over the entire visible range, that represents a total change of less than 1 unit—far too small to match the data.

The slope must be $-10$. This eliminates B and C, leaving A and D.

steps.title: Test the y-intercept at a clear point
steps.content:
The point $(3, 60)$ is clearly visible on the scatterplot.

Choice A: $y = 80 - 10(3) = 50$. Predicted value is 50; actual value is 60. Does not fit.

Choice D: $y = 90 - 10(3) = 60$. Predicted value is 60; actual value is 60. Exact match.

That matches choice D—D is correct.
